title: How Facebook's Algorithmic Tweaks Affected Engagement with News URLs over Time
snippet: Press coverage and session summary of Fabio Giglietto’s presentation at the International Communication Association (ICA) 2026 conference in Cape Town. The article details Giglietto's research findings regarding the impact of partisanship, content quality, and algorithmic tweaks on the reach of news URLs shared on Facebook.
description: An academic session summary covering Fabio Giglietto's presentation at the ICA 2026 conference on how Facebook's algorithmic tweaks affected engagement with news URLs.

title: SMSociety 2026 Conference Programme
snippet: An official event announcement for the 2026 International Conference on Social Media & Society (#SMSociety) at the University of Glasgow, listing Fabio Giglietto as a featured speaker on the 'Meta Content Library Community Roundtable: Where to for Social Media Research?' panel.
description: An announcement for the International Conference on Social Media & Society listing Professor Fabio Giglietto as an expert panelist discussing future pathways for social media research following the closure of CrowdTangle.

title: Indagine urbinate: "Così l’algoritmo influenza il voto" - Il Resto del Carlino
description: News article from Il Resto del Carlino

title: Algoritmi e democrazia, il caso Meta secondo lo studio di UniUrb
snippet: This press coverage highlights how Fabio Giglietto's computational research on Facebook algorithms became the centerpiece of an investigative broadcast on Rai3's 'Report'. It details how his findings on Meta's political content reduction policy sparked a formal inquiry in the European Parliament.
description: Press coverage highlighting Fabio Giglietto's computational research on Meta's algorithms, which was featured on Rai3's 'Report' and led to a European Parliament inquiry.

title: L'articolo 21 nella gabbia di Facebook
snippet: A deep-dive journalistic analysis of Fabio Giglietto’s study assessing how private platform policies and algorithmic filtering govern online political speech, exploring the wider implications of his findings on the freedom of speech guaranteed by Article 21 of the Italian Constitution.
description: A journalistic analysis discussing Fabio Giglietto's computational social science research on Facebook algorithmic visibility drops for Italian parliamentarians and its implications for digital freedom of speech.

title: Facebook premia gli estremisti: la ricerca dell'Università di Urbino - Lumsanews
Meno contenuti politici nei feed, la stretta di Meta è stata meno efficace contro gli estremisti - Il Sole 24 ORE
description: News article from Il Sole 24 ORE

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: An article covering a research study co-authored by Fabio Giglietto on how Meta's algorithmic choices on Facebook drastically reduced the organic reach of mainstream political figures in Italy (revealing an average 72% drop in visibility for re-elected parliamentarians), while boosting anti-EU and extremist accounts. This sparked cross-party efforts (PD and FdI) towards legislative regulation of digital platforms.
description: A news article covering a research study co-authored by Fabio Giglietto from the University of Urbino concerning the impact of Facebook's algorithm on the visibility of political discourse and extremist content.
